key_insight: RNC-LM improves Levenberg-Marquardt by using Riemann normal coordinates to add higher-order geometric corrections, making finite optimization steps more consistent with the underlying manifold curvature.
engineering_relevance: This can make nonlinear least-squares solvers more stable and accurate for curved, ill-conditioned problems in PINNs, regression, and large-scale scientific fitting.
method: Reformulates the geodesic equation in Riemann normal coordinates.
Extends geodesic acceleration to higher-order finite-step corrections.
Uses a line search along the RNC curve to control step length while preserving LM-like cost reduction.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved convergence on nonlinear least-squares benchmarks, reduction to about 1e-3 relative L2 error on a reaction-diffusion PINN failure case, and a 34-fold speedup on a large molecular potential-energy-surface fitting task.
watch_item: The higher-order update and RNC line search may add implementation complexity and require careful numerical handling in large or highly rank-deficient problems.
paper_title: Higher-Order Geometric Updates for Levenberg-Marquardt Method via Riemann Normal Coordinates
